notes: Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Objective: establish plaintiff theory that Ronaldo should resolve Yes because admitted packet shows Trump said the word in event-linked public remarks and governing NEWMENTION terms do not clearly confine 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw' to the formal stage program only. Inspected get_case/list_evidence: four case-packet items, no new submitted evidence. Read all four evidence files. Market page ev_2c7c2c8a2d76: market title 'What will Trump say during the FIFA World Cup Draw?', family PRESMENTION/NEWMENTION, strike Ronaldo, challenged No, requested Yes. Transcript ev_9cd6fa5297f3: APP/UCSB transcript titled 'Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw' dated Dec. 5, 2025; public reporter exchange at JFK Center; contains World Cup Draw/Event headers and Trump twice says standalone 'Ronaldo' in soccer/World Cup context, including 'No, but I got to meet Ronaldo...' and 'I was not Ronaldo or Messi.' Terms ev_a373371a8c9c: event/context time periods can be specific events or contexts; payout criterion is word said by entity during time period; event rules count words during portions that occur and do not require formal stage-only unless specified; technical details exclude behind-the-scenes/rehearsals/unofficial recordings unless officially broadcast/published as part of time period. Formal archive note ev_eac0f81b7818: separate Roll Call/Factba.se formal attendance transcript exists but was not retrieved/password protected; does not establish whether formal program contains or omits Ronaldo; highlights pre-event vs formal-program distinction. Native investigation: searched web for APP, Kalshi/Ronaldo market terms, formal draw archive. Opened APP source URL from packet; matched packet. Adverse/custody caveats: transcript joined in progress; note says released by Office of Communications Dec. 8, corrected Jan. 2, and portions could not be verified due to incomplete audio. These affect weight but do not negate visible Ronaldo passages. Secondary Foot Africa search result exists but not admitted and will not be cited as record proof. Later evidence targets: full market contract specification/source agency hierarchy; official White House page or APP page capture if needed; formal draw transcript/video; public video confirming sequence and venue.

Turn: openings:defendant. Inspected ready prompt, get_case, case_status, list_evidence, stat_evidence for all four packet files, and full byte ranges for all four text files. Current record: four system case-packet files, no submitted evidence/exhibits/technical reports, plaintiff opening filed. Evidence analysis: market-page proves title/family/strike/challenged resolution only; it does not include fuller market source hierarchy or a definition of event window. Pre-event reporter exchange proves transcript contains standalone Ronaldo mentions and is source-linked to presidency.ucsb.edu, but its own title says prior to the FIFA World Cup Draw, remarks joined in progress, and portions could not be verified due to incomplete audio. PRESMENTION/NEWMENTION terms prove payout requires word said by entity during time period; time period may be event/context; Exchange may specify inclusions for events; unofficial/backstage/rehearsal excluded unless officially broadcast/published as part of time period. Terms do not automatically make all venue-adjacent pre-event remarks part of the draw. Formal archive note proves a separate formal-draw transcript page exists but was not retrieved and does not establish whether formal program contains Ronaldo; it highlights the distinction between pre-event exchange and formal program. Native investigation: web search exact title found GovInfo DCPD-202501162 and PDF matching reporter-exchange transcript, useful later for provenance/admission if evidence submission opens; not cited in opening as record proof. Also found secondary discussion pages but not relied upon. Later evidence targets: GovInfo official transcript/PDF; full Kalshi market rule/source hierarchy if available; formal draw transcript/video from Roll Call/Factba.se, official FIFA/White House/GovInfo sources, or archive mirrors. Defense opening theory: concede word appears in pre-event exchange, focus on plaintiff burden to prove the exchange occurred during the market-defined FIFA World Cup Draw. Current packet shows a pre-event reporter exchange at venue, not a clear market inclusion. Record gaps and source-chain issues reduce plaintiff's present proof under preponderance.

Arguments:plaintiff work notes. After defendant opening, defense focused on missing market boundary/full rules and formal transcript. I rescanned get_case/list_evidence: no new evidence before plaintiff submissions; four original packet items plus openings. Investigated targeted sources. 1) Opened Kalshi market URL from packet. Rendered page defaulted to Canada strike but same series/event KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06. Extracted visible rules: resolves Yes if Donald Trump says [strike] as part of FIFA World Cup Draw; outcome verified from NYT, AP, Reuters, Axios, Politico, Semafor, The Information, Washington Post, WSJ, ABC, CBS, CNN, Fox News, and The White House; video primarily used; if employee consensus cannot be reached using video, transcripts of the FIFA World Cup Draw will be used according to listed publications; exact phrase/word or plural/possessive counts; if event is not opened to press for live televised/streamed event market resolves no; market closes after outcome or Dec. 5, 2025 3:26pm EST; series/event IDs shown. Submitted as ev_c3ecd221f5a8_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-c3ecd221f5a8. 2) Opened Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript URL from packet note. Page accessible, title 'Remarks: Donald Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing in Washington - December 5, 2025'; visible speaker stats and full transcript; browser/page find found no matching text for 'Ronaldo' or 'Messi'. Submitted extraction as ev_8637490aafef_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-8637490aafef. Adverse check: formal transcript absence of Ronaldo supports defense if market is formal-program-only. Plaintiff argument should concede that contingency and argue it is not the controlling boundary because Kalshi rule text says 'as part of FIFA World Cup Draw,' includes White House among sources, and general NEWMENTION terms do not clearly exclude public official pre-program event remarks. Need be careful: Kalshi extraction rendered Canada, not Ronaldo; use it only for series/event wording/source hierarchy, while Ronaldo strike identity comes from ev_2c7c2c8a2d76. No direct video captured; no White House direct page found beyond APP/UCSB packet transcript. Filing will offer original packet evidence plus the two new submitted sources, include a concise search ledger/technical report, and distinguish proof, adverse findings, and inference.

Turn: arguments:defendant. Ready opportunity arguments:defendant. Inspected current record with get_case/list_evidence and read new plaintiff evidence exact bytes. New evidence: PX-5 ev_c3ecd221f5a8 is plaintiff Kalshi rendered extraction, but it rendered Canada strike, not Ronaldo; it is relevant for same event/series wording. It says the outcome resolves Yes if Trump says the word as part of FIFA World Cup Draw, but also says video of the FIFA World Cup Draw will be primarily used, and if no consensus from video, transcripts of the FIFA World Cup Draw will be used according to listed publications. PX-6 ev_8637490aafef is plaintiff Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript extraction: formal page title Donald Trump attends World Cup Drawing; speakers/stats; visible text page-find found no Ronaldo and no Messi. Existing PX-2/pre-event transcript proves standalone Ronaldo but is titled prior to the draw, joined in progress, incomplete audio caveat. Native investigation: web searches for exact title/Ronaldo, formal draw title/Ronaldo, and KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06/Ronaldo found secondary reports and unrelated Kalshi Ronaldo markets but no stronger primary defense artifact showing pre-event exchange was in the official draw video/transcript source set. No new defense evidence submitted because plaintiff's PX-5/PX-6 already supply the decisive adverse/source-boundary evidence and outside results were not stronger primary material. Planned argument: Use admitted record only. Offer PX-5/PX-6 and key packet evidence as defense exhibits. Technical report/search ledger: defendant checked new evidence and targeted external searches, no new primary source submitted. Core merits: plaintiff cannot satisfy preponderance because market-specific source language points to video/transcripts of FIFA World Cup Draw; the formal draw transcript contains no strike; pre-event reporter exchange may be official/event-adjacent but is not shown to be part of the source-defined draw.

Rebuttal:plaintiff notes. Opponent admits strike identity and admits PX-2/pre-event reporter exchange contains standalone Ronaldo mentions. Opponent's strongest point: PX-5 says video of the FIFA World Cup Draw is primary and transcripts of the FIFA World Cup Draw are fallback; PX-6 formal Roll Call transcript has no Ronaldo; PX-2 is titled prior to the draw and has incomplete-audio/joined-in-progress caveats. Record scan: get_case/list_evidence shows no new defendant submitted evidence; defendant added only a source-search ledger. No new source submission needed. Rebuttal theory: video-primary/transcript-fallback language is an evidence hierarchy, not an event-boundary definition; it does not say only the formal Roll Call page counts or exclude official pre-program public remarks. PX-5's phrase 'as part of FIFA World Cup Draw' remains broader than formal-program-only, and includes The White House in the source list. PX-2's note says Office of Communications released/corrected the transcript, and PX-2 places the exchange at the venue, with press access, FIFA president present, about the World Cup Draw/soccer. 'Prior to' distinguishes from formal transcript but does not prove exclusion from public event context. Under preponderance, plaintiff need not eliminate every ambiguity; the better reading is inclusion absent a clear exclusion. Rebuttal will not repeat full merits; will answer source-set and burden points.

Turn: surrebuttals:defendant. Inspected get_case and list_evidence at start of opportunity. No new evidence submitted in plaintiff rebuttal; same six evidence items remain. Plaintiff rebuttal's strongest point: PX-5 video/transcripts wording is source hierarchy rather than boundary; The White House is a listed source; PX-2 is official/public/source-published and tied to event, so should count unless expressly excluded. Planned response: accept that source hierarchy verifies the Underlying, but that still confines verification to video/transcripts of the FIFA World Cup Draw. A listed White House source would matter if it were a White House transcript of the Draw; PX-2 is titled prior to the Draw and is not shown to be in draw video, draw transcript, formal program, or official presentation. Terms exclusions are not dispositive: not being backstage/rehearsal/unofficial is necessary but not sufficient; the content still must be part of the covered time period. No new native research or evidence submission because rebuttal raised interpretation of existing admitted evidence, not a new factual source claim. Surrebuttal will be concise and offered evidence will point back to already visible DX/PX evidence where useful.

Closings:plaintiff notes. Final scan with get_case/list_evidence showed same six evidence items; no new evidence or metadata changes. Defendant surrebuttal repeats that source hierarchy defines boundary, White House source only counts when documenting relevant event, relation is not inclusion, non-excluded does not imply included, and PX-6 formal no-match plus PX-2 'prior' title leave burden unmet. Closing strategy: synthesize only admitted record. Concede formal transcript no-match and provenance limits. Decisive facts: PX-1 identifies market title and Ronaldo strike; PX-2 records standalone Ronaldo in public source-published World Cup Draw-linked remarks at venue with Infantino; PX-5 says 'as part of FIFA World Cup Draw' and includes The White House in source list; PX-3 event/context terms and exclusions do not clearly exclude PX-2; PX-6 formal no-match defeats only a formal-only theory. Framing: plaintiff's reading follows 'as part of' and event/context rules; defense must add an unstated formal-program-only exclusion. Preponderance favors Yes.

Turn: closings:defendant. Defendant closing opportunity ready. Reviewed returned prompt/current record and evidence list; no new closing evidence. Plaintiff closing repeats that no clear exclusion exists, PX-2 is official/public/event-linked, and PX-3 exclusions do not cover it. Defense closing plan: synthesize burden. Proposition requires plaintiff to prove the pre-event reporter exchange counted under the market, not merely that the market lacks an express exclusion. Evidence weights: market page/PX-1 proves strike and title; PX-5 supplies rule text but rendered Canada, and its strongest defense point is video of FIFA World Cup Draw primary and transcripts of FIFA World Cup Draw fallback; PX-6 is the only formal draw transcript test and no Ronaldo/Messi found; PX-2 proves Ronaldo in separate transcript titled prior to draw, with joined-in-progress and incomplete-audio caveats; PX-3 terms require covered time period and do not make every non-excluded official remark automatically included. Strongest contrary point: broad 'as part of' plus venue/FIFA/official transcript. Response: those facts prove connection, not inclusion in the resolving source/event. No new evidence or technical reports allowed/needed in closing.
